title: Oracle severed 21,000 employees during an AI spending spree, then hit a $7 billion Wisconsin power hurdle
excerpt: The company’s ambitions helped weaken its credit rating, leaving Oracle one notch above junk status and facing a massive financial requirement.
content_html:
The race to dominate artificial intelligence is becoming one of the most expensive corporate contests in history.

Amazon, Microsoft, Google parent Alphabet and Meta are expected to spend about $600 billion on AI infrastructure in 2026, according to Reuters. The historic spending spree is squeezing cash flow and putting pressure on companies to prove that their investments in chips, servers and data centers will eventually pay off.

Must Read

  • Jeff Bezos backs a platform that lets anyone invest in rental homes for as little as $100 — 6 ways to build wealth like a landlord without actually being one

  • Dave Ramsey warns nearly 50% of Americans are making 1 big Social Security mistake — here's what it is and 3 simple steps to fix it ASAP

  • Millionaires under 43 hold only 25% of their wealth in stocks. Here's where their money is actually going

Oracle has made one of the biggest bets. The company has emerged as a major supplier of AI computing capacity after reportedly signing a $300 billion contract with OpenAI, but the data centers needed to fulfill that agreement have contributed to a cash crunch. Oracle subsequently pursued thousands of job cuts as it looked for ways to fund its expansion, Reuters reported in March.

By the end of its 2026 fiscal year, Oracle's workforce had fallen by approximately 21,000 employees, or 13%, from 162,000 to 141,000. The reduction came as Oracle restructured its business, partly in response to the adoption of AI within its operations.

Now, Oracle's weakened financial position could make it significantly more expensive to secure electricity for one of the data centers at the heart of its AI ambitions.

Wisconsin regulators want a $7 billion guarantee

Oracle is involved in a planned nearly one-gigawatt data center in Port Washington, Wisconsin, that is expected to help the company fulfill its OpenAI contract.

But the Public Service Commission of Wisconsin has declined to loosen financial safeguards designed to prevent residential electricity customers from bearing the costs if a massive data center fails or closes, according to the Financial Times.

Under We Energies' "very large customer" tariff, data center operators with an S&P credit rating below A- must provide collateral covering the power plants and transmission infrastructure constructed to serve them.

Oracle was rated BBB, two notches below the threshold, when the requirements were considered. S&P subsequently downgraded the company to BBB-, leaving it one notch above junk status. The ratings agency cited Oracle's heavy AI spending and uncertain path toward generating enough profits from those investments.

Amazon, Microsoft, Google parent Alphabet and Meta are expected to spend about $600 billion on AI infrastructure in 2026, according to Reuters. The historic spending spree is squeezing cash flow and putting pressure on companies to prove that their investments in chips, servers and data centers will eventually pay off. Here's where their money is actually going Oracle has made one of the biggest bets. The company has emerged as a major supplier of AI computing capacity after reportedly signing a $300 billion contract with OpenAI, but the data centers needed to fulfill that agreement have contributed to a cash crunch. Oracle subsequently pursued thousands of job cuts as it looked for ways to fund its expansion, Reuters reported in March. By the end of its 2026 fiscal year, Oracle's workforce had fallen by approximately 21,000 employees, or 13%, from 162,000 to 141,000. The reduction came as Oracle restructured its business, partly in response to the adoption of AI within its operations. Now, Oracle's weakened financial position could make it significantly more expensive to secure electricity for one of the data centers at the heart of its AI ambitions. Wisconsin regulators want a $7 billion guarantee Oracle is involved in a planned nearly one-gigawatt data center in Port Washington, Wisconsin, that is expected to help the company fulfill its OpenAI contract. But the Public Service Commission of Wisconsin has declined to loosen financial safeguards designed to prevent residential electricity customers from bearing the costs if a massive data center fails or closes, according to the Financial Times. Under We Energies' "very large customer" tariff, data center operators with an S&P credit rating below A- must provide collateral covering the power plants and transmission infrastructure constructed to serve them. Oracle was rated BBB, two notches below the threshold, when the requirements were considered. S&P subsequently downgraded the company to BBB-, leaving it one notch above junk status. The ratings agency cited Oracle's heavy AI spending and uncertain path toward generating enough profits from those investments.

sentences.zh: 根据 We Energies 的“大客户”关税，标准普尔信用评级低于 A- 的数据中心运营商必须提供抵押品，涵盖为其服务而建造的发电厂和传输基础设施。

blocks.html_en: Oracle was rated BBB, two notches below the threshold, when the requirements were considered. S&P subsequently downgraded the company to BBB-, leaving it one notch above junk status. The ratings agency cited Oracle's heavy AI spending and uncertain path toward generating enough profits from those investments.

sentences.zh: 在考虑这些要求时，Oracle 被评为 BBB，比阈值低两个等级。

sentences.en: S&P subsequently downgraded the company to BBB-, leaving it one notch above junk status.
sentences.zh: 标准普尔随后将该公司的评级下调至 BBB-，使其比垃圾级评级高一级。

sentences.en: The ratings agency cited Oracle's heavy AI spending and uncertain path toward generating enough profits from those investments.
sentences.zh: 该评级机构指出，甲骨文在人工智能领域投入巨额资金，而且从这些投资中获得足够利润的道路还不确定。

content_html_zh:
主导人工智能的竞赛正在成为历史上最昂贵的企业竞赛之一。

据路透社报道，亚马逊、微软、谷歌母公司 Alphabet 和 Meta 预计到 2026 年将在人工智能基础设施上花费约 6000 亿美元。 历史性的支出热潮正在挤压现金流，并给公司带来压力，要求他们证明他们在芯片、服务器和数据中心的投资最终会得到回报。

必读

  • 杰夫·贝索斯 (Jeff Bezos) 支持一个平台，让任何人都可以以低至 100 美元的价格投资出租房屋——无需真正成为房东即可像房东一样积累财富的 6 种方法
  • Dave Ramsey 警告说，近 50% 的美国人在社会保障方面犯了 1 个重大错误——这就是它的本质，以及尽快修复它的 3 个简单步骤
  • 43岁以下的百万富翁仅将其财富的25%投资于股票。 这就是他们的钱实际去向的地方

甲骨文下了最大的赌注之一。 据报道，在与 OpenAI 签署了价值 3000 亿美元的合同后，该公司已成为人工智能计算能力的主要供应商，但履行该协议所需的数据中心却导致了现金短缺。 据路透社 3 月份报道，甲骨文随后裁减了数千名员工，以寻找为其扩张提供资金的方法。

截至 2026 财年末，甲骨文员工数量减少了约 21,000 人，即 13%，从 162,000 人减少至 141,000 人。 此次裁员是在甲骨文重组其业务之际进行的，部分原因是为了在其运营中采用人工智能。

现在，甲骨文财务状况的疲软可能会使其其人工智能雄心勃勃的核心数据中心之一的电力供应成本大幅增加。

威斯康星州监管机构希望获得 70 亿美元的担保

甲骨文参与了威斯康星州华盛顿港计划建设的近 1 吉瓦的数据中心，预计将帮助该公司履行其 OpenAI 合同。

但据英国《金融时报》报道，威斯康星州公共服务委员会拒绝放松旨在防止住宅电力客户在大型数据中心发生故障或关闭时承担费用的财务保障措施。

根据 We Energies 的“大客户”关税，标准普尔信用评级低于 A- 的数据中心运营商必须提供抵押品，涵盖为其服务而建造的发电厂和传输基础设施。

在考虑这些要求时，Oracle 被评为 BBB，比阈值低两个等级。 标准普尔随后将该公司的评级下调至 BBB-，使其比垃圾级评级高一级。 该评级机构指出，甲骨文在人工智能领域投入巨额资金，而且从这些投资中获得足够利润的道路还不确定。
